summary: The task is complete in substance. `/app/settings` is implemented as a native owner-workspace surface, `Settings` is present in the private nav, title/description persist through the singleton site-settings boundary, demo users are read-only in both UI and server action enforcement, and shared branding is sourced from site settings in the root layout metadata and shell header. The required checks in the task contract already passed.
satisfied_exit_criteria: The owner can edit title and description from `/app/settings` via the shipped settings form and server action.
Saved values propagate through the shared shell branding and browser metadata by reading `getSiteSettings()` in the root layout.
Demo users can inspect the settings route but cannot save: controls are disabled/read-only in the UI and server-side writes are rejected.
`npm run test:e2e -- --grep settings` passed in the provided deterministic check summary.
`npm run verify` passed in the provided deterministic check summary.
